user_query:
I'm the long-time tech helper for our AA home group. Our little Python script that generates the meeting schedule for our website/email is mislabeling formats and seems to ignore the configured output folder. A newcomer pointed out that Monday's online meeting was showing up as in-person. Please debug and fix it so our schedule is accurate and the outputs land where they're supposed to.

You'll find everything in this workspace:
- Input data: input/meetings.csv
- Config: config/config.json
- Script to fix: src/generate_schedule.py

Please make the following concrete changes and deliverables:
1) Fix src/generate_schedule.py (do not change input/meetings.csv headers) so that it reads input/meetings.csv and config/config.json and writes two outputs to the directory defined by config["output_dir"] (currently "output"):
   - output/schedule.json — an array of meeting objects including only rows where status == "active". Each object must have exactly these fields and formats:
     • id (string)
     • name (string)
     • day (one of Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday; title-cased)
     • start_time (24-hour HH:MM, zero-padded)
     • duration_min (integer)
     • format (normalized to either "online" or "in-person" regardless of input capitalization or variations like "inperson")
     • location (string; empty string allowed)
     • notes (include only if config["include_notes"] is true; empty string allowed)
     • timezone (string from config["default_timezone"]) 
   - output/summary.csv — a CSV with header exactly: day,format,count. Each row is the count of meetings grouped by day and format taken from schedule.json (so counts must exactly match the schedule). Exclude non-active meetings from both files.

   Additional correctness constraints:
   - Respect config["output_dir"] for both outputs; do not hard-code paths.
   - Use the actual CSV headers (start_time, duration_min, meeting_format, etc.); do not rename the input columns.
   - Normalize meeting_format values so only "online" or "in-person" appear in schedule.json and summary.csv.

2) Save a brief plain-text email to output/fix_email.txt addressed to our Group Service Team that:
   - Explains the root cause(s) you fixed (e.g., delimiter/field mismatches, wrong config keys, output path handling).
   - States the total number of active meetings and provides the breakdown by day and format based on output/summary.csv.
   - Confirms that corrected files (schedule.json and summary.csv) are saved under the configured output directory.
   - Keeps a supportive tone suitable for AA service and stays under 200 words.

Please keep the input files unchanged. Modify src/generate_schedule.py and, only if absolutely necessary for correctness, config/config.json, but prefer fixing code to read the existing config keys. The final artifacts I expect to see are:
- output/schedule.json
- output/summary.csv
- output/fix_email.txt
All three must be consistent with one another. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
